weeks.weekly_summary: Foundation week with 2 easy runs on preferred days (Mon/Wed). Focus on aerobic base building and establishing consistent routine. Low intensity, high consistency approach.
weeks.coaching_notes: Recent activity shows ~10km/week with easy paces (5:50-6:10). Starting conservatively at 22km/week respects the 4-hour budget (240 min) while building base. No hard sessions in Week 1—pure aerobic foundation. Cross-training (Football, Gym, Pilates) is blocked; respect those commitments.
weeks.next_week_preview: Week 2 introduces first quality session (tempo run or fartlek intervals) on Monday or Wednesday. Volume increases to 28km.

weeks.coaching_notes: Week 2 marks introduction of polarized training. One quality session (20% of volume) balances two easy runs (80%). Tempo run on Monday builds lactate threshold; Wednesday easy run allows recovery. Progressive volume increase from Week 1 (22km → 28km, ~27% increase—monitor for fatigue).
weeks.next_week_preview: Week 3 increases long run to ~15km on Wednesday and maintains tempo/fartlek quality on Monday. Total volume ~32km.

days.title: Tempo Fartlek 9km (4×3min@tempo)
days.description: Warm up 2km easy, then 4×3min at tempo pace (85-90% max HR, comfortably hard) with 2min recovery jogs between. Cool down 1km easy. Builds lactate threshold and aerobic power.
days.notes: First quality session of block. Keep effort controlled—should be able to speak few words but not sing.

days.title: 🧘 Pilates
days.description: Pilates session (fixed commitment).
days.notes: Blocked cross-training day. Light activity pairs well after tempo session.

days.title: Easy 14km
days.description: Longer easy run building endurance base. Maintain conversational pace. Focus on steady, controlled effort.
days.notes: Recovery run after Monday's tempo effort. Build aerobic capacity.

weeks.weekly_summary: Volume peaks at 32km with sustained quality work. Tempo intervals on Monday, longer easy run (15km) on Wednesday. Consolidating aerobic base before transition to structured speed work.
weeks.coaching_notes: Week 3 maintains 80/20 split. Tempo run progresses (5×3min vs. 4×3min Week 2) to build lactate threshold capacity. Long run increases to 15km—still at easy pace, building half-marathon endurance foundation. Total volume ~32km respects 4-hour budget (230 min running). Monitor cumulative fatigue; if HRV declining or perceived exertion high, dial back slightly in Week 4.
weeks.next_week_preview: Week 4 reduces volume to 26km (taper prep for next block). Maintains quality on Monday but shorter long run (12km). Prepares for transition into Aerobic Development phase.

days.title: Tempo Intervals 10km (5×3min@tempo)
days.description: Warm up 2km easy, then 5×3min at tempo pace (85-90% max HR) with 2min recovery jogs. Cool down 1km easy. Builds lactate threshold and submaximal speed.
days.notes: Progression from Week 2: increased reps (5 vs. 4). Maintain discipline in pace control.

days.title: 🧘 Pilates
days.description: Pilates session (fixed commitment).

days.title: Easy 15km (long run)
days.description: Longest run of block at easy, conversational pace. Building half-marathon endurance base. Maintain rhythm and consistent breathing. Can include 2-3 strides (20-30sec at near-race pace) in final 2km to prepare neuromuscularly.
days.notes: Peak long run for Base Building phase. Critical for building half-marathon aerobic capacity.

weeks.weekly_summary: Transition week: volume reduces to 26km (preparation for next block intensity). Tempo run maintained at 9km; long run scaled back to 12km. Foundation laid for progression into VO2 max and marathon-pace work.
weeks.coaching_notes: Week 4 marks soft transition into Aerobic Development phase (next 4-week block will emphasize VO2 max and race-pace workouts). Current week reduces volume ~19% from Week 3 peak—prevents overtraining and allows nervous system recovery. Maintain tempo quality but shorter duration (9km vs. 10km). Long run back to 12km—sustainable volume entering higher-intensity phase. Recent training load manageable; no fatigue flags detected. Athlete in good position to build speed next block.
weeks.next_week_preview: Following 4-week block (Week 5 onward): increase tempo duration to 10-12km, introduce VO2 max intervals (6-8×3min @ 92-95% max HR), and progress long-run format to include marathon-pace segments. Periodization continues through peak training phase (Weeks 9-12) before taper.

coaching_overview:
**4-Week Base Building & Aerobic Development Block (June 25 – July 22)**

**Summary:** This block establishes the aerobic foundation for your half-marathon goal (2:00, ~5:44/km pace). With only 2 sessions/week and a 4-hour budget, the plan maximizes time efficiency using polarized training (80% easy, 20% quality).

**Phase Progression:**
- **Weeks 1–3: Base Building** — Pure aerobic development, one quality session introduced in Week 2, progressively building long-run distance (10→14→15km)
- **Week 4: Aerobic Development (transition)** — Soft entry into next phase; volume reduction prepares for higher-intensity VO2 max work

**Key Workouts:**
- **Tempo/Fartlek (Mondays):** Lactate threshold development. Started conservatively (4×3min), progressed to 5×3min (Week 3), then backed off (4×3min Week 4) to manage fatigue.
- **Easy Runs (Wednesdays):** Long-run progression building endurance. Peak at 15km (Week 3)—critical for half-marathon base.
- **Cross-Training (Fixed):** Friday Gym, Saturday Football, Tuesday Pilates are blocked and respected; allow active recovery alongside running.

**Volume:** 22 → 28 → 32 → 26km (peak Week 3, then intentional reduction for recovery).

**Intensity Distribution:** 80% Zone 1-2 (easy), 20% Zone 3-4 (tempo/fartlek) across block.

**Periodization Note:** After this block, progress into Weeks 5–8 (Aerobic Development Phase) with VO2 max intervals, longer tempos, and race-specific pacing. Follow structured periodization through Peak Training (Weeks 9–12) and eventual taper (Weeks 13–15) leading to your half-marathon race.

**Recovery Flags:** None detected. Recent Strava data shows ~10km/week; plan ramps progressively (22km W1 is 2.2× load increase). Monitor subjective fatigue and HRV if available. Adjust Week 4–5 intensity if cumulative fatigue emerges.

**Next Steps:** Complete this block, assess fitness (easy run pace, tempo feel), then enter structured Aerobic Development phase with VO2 max emphasis.
